intro: Ferdinandusa elliptica, also known as Ferdinandusa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
